message:
perf(ingest): cut steady-state writer overhead for batched inserts

Three coordinated changes that remove per-conversation overhead from
the begin-concurrent ingest path. Profile traces during a remote-source
re-index showed the inner loop spending more time on bookkeeping
(preflight write, redundant agent/workspace upserts, redundant source
upserts, PRAGMA database_list lookups) than on the actual conversation
insert. None of those bookkeeping steps were needed on the steady-state
path, only on the first writer-open or when the row content actually
changed.

1. One-shot ephemeral-writer preflight instead of every persist
   (`src/indexer/mod.rs` + `src/storage/sqlite.rs`):

   `persist_chunked_with_writer_path` was issuing
   `UPDATE meta SET value = value WHERE key = 'schema_version'` on
   every single chunk to surface a clear "readonly DB" error early
   (CASS #162 item 2). On a 10k-conversation re-index, that's 10k
   trivial writes that just bump WAL frames for no diagnostic gain
   after the first one.

   `FrankenStorage` now carries an `AtomicBool ephemeral_writer_-
   preflight_verified` flag (initialized to `false`). The first
   successful preflight calls
   `mark_ephemeral_writer_preflight_verified()`; subsequent persists
   on the same storage handle skip the no-op write entirely. The
   diagnostic still triggers on a freshly opened, broken handle —
   which is exactly the case that originally motivated it.

   Also drops the per-chunk
   `ensure_embedded_source_registered(franken, &conv.metadata, ...)`
   call from inside the `with_concurrent_retry` body. That helper
   has been deleted; provenance is registered on the outer batch
   path before the retry loop, so re-running it on every retry was
   pure overhead. The `LOCAL_SOURCE_ID` import is now `#[cfg(test)]`
   only because nothing else in `persist::` references it.

   `database_path()` now returns the cached `db_path` field instead
   of issuing `PRAGMA database_list;` and walking rows. The path is
   captured at `FrankenStorage::new(conn, path)` and never changes
   for a given handle.

2. Skip writes when `ensure_agent` / `ensure_workspace` /
   `upsert_source` find an identical row (`src/storage/sqlite.rs`):

   The old shape was UPDATE-then-INSERT-fallback-on-zero-rows-
   affected, plus an additional retry on `UniqueViolation`. That
   wrote the row even when name/version/kind were byte-identical,
   bumping `updated_at` every time and bloating the WAL.

   New shape uses `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(slug/path/id) DO UPDATE
   SET ... WHERE NOT (col1 IS excluded.col1 AND col2 IS ...)`. The
   `WHERE NOT (...)` guard makes the UPDATE a no-op when every
   tracked column already matches, so a re-index of unchanged
   metadata produces zero WAL frames for these tables.

   Specifically:
   - `ensure_agent` — upsert keyed on `slug`, guarded on
     `name`/`version`/`kind`. The post-upsert `SELECT id` no longer
     uses `NOT INDEXED` / `ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1` since the
     unique constraint already pins exactly one row per slug.
   - `ensure_workspace` — split into two paths: `display_name = Some`
     uses guarded `ON CONFLICT(path) DO UPDATE` so we don't blow
     away an existing label; `display_name = None` uses
     `INSERT OR IGNORE` so we never overwrite a labeled row with
     `NULL`.
   - `upsert_source` — guard checks all five tracked columns
     (`kind`, `host_label`, `machine_id`, `platform`, `config_json`).
     `created_at` is preserved via `excluded.*` only when an actual
     update fires; for unchanged rows the original row's
     `created_at` and `updated_at` both stay put.

   All `FrankenStorage` constructors funnel through a single
   `FrankenStorage::new(conn, path)` helper (replacing the inline
   `Self { conn }` literals at every open call site) so the new
   `db_path` and `AtomicBool` fields are always initialized
   correctly. In-memory test fixture passes `PathBuf::from(":memory:")`.

3. Tests + bench coverage (`src/storage/sqlite.rs` +
   `benches/integration_regression.rs`):

   - `ensure_agent_unchanged_preserves_updated_at` — call
     `ensure_agent(...)` twice with the same agent across a 5ms
     gap; assert `updated_at` is byte-identical the second time
     (i.e. the guarded UPDATE didn't fire).
   - `upsert_source_unchanged_preserves_updated_at` — same shape
     for `upsert_source`. Asserts every tracked column plus
     `created_at`/`updated_at` are unchanged across the second call.
   - New `bench_insert_remote_source_reuse` benchmark in
     `benches/integration_regression.rs`. Adds
     `generate_remote_conversation` / `make_remote_conversation`
     helpers that pin `cass.origin.source_id = "bench-remote-source"`
     and `host = "bench-remote-host"`, then drives both the
     persist-pipeline and direct-FrankenStorage paths through
     repeated inserts that share the same source. Wired into the
     `criterion_group!` target list. The bench is the load shape
     this commit is tuned for and will exercise the no-op upserts
     on the inner loop.

No behavior change for first-time inserts or genuine row updates;
the savings come from short-circuiting redundant work that the
previous implementation always paid for.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
